# Attachable

**Protocol**

A protocol describing a type that can be attached to a test report or written to disk when a test is run.

## Declaration

```swift
protocol Attachable : ~Copyable
```

## Overview

To attach an attachable value to a test, pass it to [doc://org.swift.testing/documentation/Testing/Attachment/record(_:named:sourceLocation:)]. To further configure an attachable value before you attach it, use it to initialize an instance of [doc://org.swift.testing/documentation/Testing/Attachment] and set its properties before passing it to [doc://org.swift.testing/documentation/Testing/Attachment/record(_:sourceLocation:)]. An attachable value can only be attached to a test once.

The testing library provides default conformances to this protocol for a variety of standard library types. Most user-defined types do not need to conform to this protocol.

A type should conform to this protocol if it can be represented as a sequence of bytes that would be diagnostically useful if a test fails. If a type cannot conform directly to this protocol (such as a non-final class or a type declared in a third-party module), you can create a wrapper type that conforms to [doc://org.swift.testing/documentation/Testing/AttachableWrapper] to act as a proxy.

## Instance Properties

- **estimatedAttachmentByteCount**: An estimate of the number of bytes of memory needed to store this value as an attachment.

## Instance Methods

- **preferredName(for:basedOn:)**: Generate a preferred name for the given attachment.
- **withUnsafeBytes(for:_:)**: Call a function and pass a buffer representing this instance to it.

## Attaching values to tests

- **Attachment**: A type describing values that can be attached to the output of a test run and inspected later by the user.
- **AttachableWrapper**: A protocol describing a type that can be attached to a test report or written to disk when a test is run and which contains another value that it stands in for.

## Inherited By

- AttachableWrapper
